title: VREM
description: Remove an element from a vector set.
complexity: O(log(N)) for each element removed, where N is the number of elements in the vector set.

params.description: the name of the key that holds the vector set
params.name: key

params.description: the name of the element to remove from the vector set
params.name: element

returns.description: true if the element was removed, false if either element or key do not exist

params.name: element

nredisstack_sync.signature: VectorSetRemove(RedisKey key, RedisValue member, CommandFlags flags = CommandFlags.None)

nredisstack_async.signature: VectorSetRemoveAsync(RedisKey key, RedisValue member, CommandFlags flags = CommandFlags.None)

content:
Remove an element from a vector set. ```shell VADD vset VALUES 3 1 0 1 bar (integer) 1 VREM vset bar (integer) 1 VREM vset bar (integer) 0 ``` `VREM` reclaims memory immediately. It does not use tombstones or logical deletions, making it safe to use in long-running applications that frequently update the same vector set. ## Required arguments
key is the name of the key that holds the vector set.
element is the name of the element to remove from the vector set.
## Related topics - [Vector sets]({{< relref "/develop/data-types/vector-sets" >}}) ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
❌ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
❌ Active-Active | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="vrem-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): 0 if either element or key do not exist; 1 if the element was removed. -tab-sep- [Boolean reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#booleans): false if either element or key do not exist; true if the element was removed. {{< /multitabs >}}
intro:
Remove an element from a vector set.

```shell
VADD vset VALUES 3 1 0 1 bar
(integer) 1

VREM vset bar
(integer) 1

VREM vset bar
(integer) 0
```

`VREM` reclaims memory immediately. It does not use tombstones or logical deletions, making it safe to use in long-running applications that frequently update the same vector set.

sections.content:
Remove an element from a vector set.

```shell
VADD vset VALUES 3 1 0 1 bar
(integer) 1

VREM vset bar
(integer) 1

VREM vset bar
(integer) 0
```

`VREM` reclaims memory immediately. It does not use tombstones or logical deletions, making it safe to use in long-running applications that frequently update the same vector set.

key is the name of the key that holds the vector set.
element is the name of the element to remove from the vector set.

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
{{< multitabs id="vrem-return-info" 
    tab1="RESP2" 
    tab2="RESP3" >}}

[Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): 0 if either element or key do not exist; 1 if the element was removed.

-tab-sep-

[Boolean reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#booleans): false if either element or key do not exist; true if the element was removed.

{{< /multitabs >}}
